blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
If you are looking to play **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**) on Android, several apps on the Google Play Store bring this classic psychological card game to your phone. 

The best Android apps for Blind Man's Bluff include:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game** (by hheeee)
* **Description:** This is a single-player, offline version of Blind Man's Bluff. The app challenges you to win when you can't see your own hand. You must look at the AI opponent's card, read their "tells," and decide whether to bet, fold, or bluff across multiple difficulty levels. It is a great option if you want to practice your strategy solo.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Description:** Designed as a local multiplayer experience, this app allows you to play the classic forehead card game with friends and family. The gameplay focuses heavily on the psychological aspect of the game—you look at your opponent's card, read their facial expressions, and try to predict your victory without knowing your own card. 

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Description:** A straightforward, lightweight simulation app that lets you play the game using virtual playing cards. It is designed to act as a digital deck for multiple people playing together in the same room. 

### 4. **Bluetooth Indian Poker** (by Haksoft)
* **Description:** If you want to play with a friend nearby without sharing a single screen, this app allows two players to connect their Android devices via Bluetooth. You can play the classic "card on the forehead" game completely free.

---

### *Note on Similar App Store Names:*
* **Teen Patti Apps:** If you search for "Indian Poker" on the Play Store, you will see many popular apps like *Teen Patti Gold* or *Teen Patti Octro*. While sometimes translated as "Indian Poker", **Teen Patti** is actually a 3-card brag game where players usually look at their own cards, which is different from the "card-on-forehead" mechanics of Blind Man's Bluff.
* **Heads Up! / Charades Apps:** If you are looking for the party game where you hold the phone to your forehead and guess words based on your friends' clues, search for **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros.) or **Charades**.

forehead poker app Android.response:
Depending on what you mean by "forehead poker," you might be looking for the **classic card game** (often called *Indian Poker* or *Blind Man's Poker*, where you see everyone's card except your own) or a **forehead-based party guessing game** (like *Heads Up!*). 

The best Android apps for both styles of "forehead poker" games include:

### 1. Indian Poker (The Card Game)
In these apps, you play the actual card game where a card is "held to your forehead" digitally—meaning you can see your opponents' cards, but you must bet and bluff without knowing your own.

*   **Indian Poker - Card Game** *(by Wooah Tech Inc.)*
    *   **What it is:** A dedicated mobile adaptation of the classic forehead poker game. 
    *   **How it works:** You play against friends, family, or AI. The app hides your card from you while showing you your opponent's card, forcing you to read their facial expressions, manage your psychology, and bluff your way to victory.
*   **Indian poker** *(by Kanbe)*
    *   **What it is:** A lightweight, straightforward simulation of the classic game.
    *   **How it works:** Designed specifically for local multiplayer, it acts as a digital deck of cards for groups wanting to play the forehead card game together without needing physical cards.
*   **Anipang Poker / 애니팡 포커** *(by Playlinks)*
    *   **What it is:** A highly popular social casino app.
    *   **How it works:** While it features traditional poker variants like 7-Card Stud, it also includes a dedicated, fully-realized **"Indian Poker"** multiplayer mode where you can compete against other online players in the classic forehead-style format.

---

### 2. Forehead Party Games (Word Guessing)
If you are looking for the popular party game format where you hold the phone to your forehead and guess words based on your friends' clues, these are the top choices:

*   **Heads Up!** *(by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)*
    *   **What it is:** The definitive forehead-guessing game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres.
    *   **How it works:** You select a category (celebrities, movies, animals, etc.), place the phone on your forehead facing your friends, and try to guess the word on the screen before the timer runs out using their clues. Tilting the phone down marks a correct guess, while tilting it up passes.
*   **Forehead Fanatic** *(by Revont Oy)*
    *   **What it is:** A fast-paced, group-friendly party game designed specifically for game nights.
    *   **How it works:** You slap the phone on your forehead and try to guess who or what you are. It features dozens of diverse themes and supports anywhere from 2 to over 20 players, making it perfect for large gatherings.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The card game mechanic where you cannot see your own cards (but everyone else can) is most famously used in **Indian Poker** (also known as **Blind Man's Bluff** or **Forehead Poker**) and the cooperative game **Hanabi**. 

Several real, existing Android apps on the Google Play Store let you play these games:

### 1. Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff Apps
In these games, you hold your card up to your forehead (or the app hides your card while showing your opponents') so you have to bet based entirely on the cards you can see on other players' heads.

*   **Indian Poker - Card Game** *(by Wooah Tech Inc.)*: This app lets you play the classic forehead poker game with friends and family. You must predict your victory and read your opponents' psychological tells using only the cards you can see in their hands.
*   **Blindex** *(by Blindex)*: A dedicated, free, offline single-player version of Blind Man's Bluff. It simulates the "card on your forehead" mechanic against AI opponents, allowing you to read their tells and bet across multiple difficulty levels.
*   **Indian poker** *(by Kanbe)*: A simple, multiplayer-focused simulation of the classic card-on-forehead game designed to be played with multiple people.

### 2. Hanabi (Cooperative "Blind" Card Game)
In Hanabi, players work together to launch a grand fireworks display. The twist is that you hold your cards facing outward—you can see everyone else's cards, but you have no idea what is in your own hand and must rely on clues from your teammates.

*   **Board Game Arena** *(by Board Game Arena)*: The official Board Game Arena Android app features a fully licensed, digital version of **Hanabi**. You can play in real-time or turn-based modes with players from all over the world.
*   **Hanabi Tracker** *(by André Rhine-Davis)*: If you prefer playing the physical tabletop version of Hanabi with friends, this highly-rated companion app is incredibly useful. It acts as a digital notepad to help you track the clues and negative information your teammates give you about your hidden hand.

### 3. Party Game Alternative
*   **Heads Up!** *(by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)*: While not a traditional playing-card game, this is the ultimate digital party game built entirely around this mechanic. You hold your phone to your forehead so you can't see the card on the screen, and your friends must shout clues to help you guess the word before the timer runs out.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The popular party game where you hold your phone to your forehead while friends give you clues is commonly referred to as a "heads up" or "charades" game.

This is the official, incredibly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are the best real, offline-capable single-player bluffing card games (and one classic bluffing dice game) available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
* **Description:** This app brings the classic card game of deception (also known as Cheat, BS, or I Doubt It) to your phone. You play cards face-down and declare their rank, but you are allowed to lie. If a CPU opponent calls your bluff and catches you, you take the penalty; if they are wrong, they do. 
* **Offline Features:** It is fully optimized for offline play, allowing you to engage in intense 4-player battles against three smart CPU opponents. It also features unique strategic items like a "Lie Counter" and "Bluff Shield" to mix up the gameplay.

### 2. **Bluff Card Game** (by OENGINES GAMES)
* **Description:** A highly rated, dedicated offline version of the classic "I Doubt It" / "Cheat" card game. The objective is simple: be the first to get rid of all your cards by successfully bluffing your way through your turns.
* **Offline Features:** Built specifically for "no-WiFi" situations. It features a 2-player mode (1v1 against a smart AI) and a 4-player mode with multiple unpredictable computer opponents. The AI has dynamic difficulty to keep the game challenging as you improve.

### 3. **CleverFox - I Doubt It**
* **Description:** Another excellent adaptation of the "Cheat" card game. It features a sleek, user-friendly interface and allows you to customize the rules. You can choose from four different play options (same rank, lower card, upper card, or random card) to add a layer of strategy.
* **Offline Features:** Offers a robust offline mode where you can challenge computer-controlled opponents with varying playstyles.

### 4. **Coup** (or **Coup board game**)
* **Description:** A faithful mobile adaptation of the wildly popular tabletop bluffing game. In *Coup*, you hold two secret character cards, each with unique abilities (like stealing, assassinating, or blocking). You can claim to have any character you want, but if someone calls your bluff and you can't prove it, you lose influence.
* **Offline Features:** While it has online multiplayer, the app includes a dedicated offline "Practice" mode where you can play solo against AI opponents to hone your deception and deduction skills.

### 5. **Liar's Dice** (by Hamilton Digital Media Limited)
* **Description:** While technically a dice game rather than a card game, *Liar's Dice* (also known as Perudo or Dudo) is the ultimate classic bluffing game. Players roll their dice in secret and take turns bidding on how many dice of a certain face are on the table, bluffing and calling out "liar" when they suspect a fake bid.
* **Offline Features:** This version is highly offline-friendly and designed for quick, one-handed play. You can play solo against computer opponents that have distinct personalities and decision-making styles.

best offline single player card game And.response:
Here are some of the best offline, single-player card games available on the Google Play Store, ranging from roguelike deckbuilders to tactical puzzle card games:

### 1. **Slay the Spire**
The gold standard of roguelike deckbuilders. You choose one of four unique characters and attempt to climb an ever-changing spire. Along the way, you draft cards, fight bizarre monsters, and collect powerful relics that synergize to create game-breaking combos. It is a premium game with zero microtransactions, offering hundreds of hours of deep strategic gameplay entirely offline.

### 2. **Balatro**
A massive hit poker-themed roguelike deckbuilder. In *Balatro*, you play traditional poker hands to score points, but the real strategy lies in buying and combining unique "Joker" cards. These Jokers trigger wild multipliers, alter card values, and completely break the rules of standard poker. It is incredibly addictive, runs flawlessly offline, and is perfect for quick or long sessions.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
A premium, dark-fantasy deckbuilder designed specifically for mobile. It features a comfortable portrait-mode layout (perfect for one-handed play) and combines deep RPG mechanics with card combat. You can choose from various classes (like Warrior, Rogue, or Mage) and customize your deck with hundreds of cards and weapons as you explore a rich story. 

### 4. **Night of the Full Moon**
A beautifully illustrated, dark fairy-tale-themed card game based on the story of Little Red Riding Hood. You choose a class, build your deck, and engage in turn-based card battles against spooky forest creatures. The game is played in portrait mode, works completely offline, and offers a generous amount of free content with optional paid expansions for extra classes and storylines.

### 5. **Card Thief**
A brilliant mix of solitaire and stealth puzzle mechanics. You play as a thief represented by a card on a 3x3 grid. Your goal is to sneak through the grid, extinguishing torches, pickpocketing guards, and stealing treasure without getting caught. It requires careful planning, resource management, and tactical pathfinding, making it a highly rewarding offline puzzle game.

### 6. **Pirates Outlaws**
If you enjoy *Slay the Spire* but want a different theme, *Pirates Outlaws* is an excellent choice. You navigate a pirate ship through various maps, managing your "ammo" (which acts as your action points) to play cards and defeat outlaws. It features a colorful art style, over a dozen playable characters with distinct mechanics, and multiple offline game modes.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:

If you prefer a multiplayer version of the bluffing card game (often called “Bluff” or “Bluff Online”), you can download **Bluff Online** by Magic Board from Google Play [3]. This app supports online multiplayer with friends and worldwide players, where the goal is to get rid of your cards by bluffing [1][3].

| App Name         | Type                     | Key Features                                                                 | Source |
|------------------|--------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------|
| **Blindex**      | Single-player, offline   | Free, AI dealer, Indian Poker style, Blind Man’s Bluff rules                 | [4]    |
| **Bluff Online** | Multiplayer, online      | Play with friends/world, bluff/deceive, get rid of cards, challenge bluffs   | [1][3] |

Both apps are available on Android. **Blindex** is ideal if you want a solo experience with the exact “Blind Man’s Bluff” rules, while **Bluff Online** is better for competitive multiplayer bluffing.

forehead poker app Android.response:
There is no app called “forehead poker,” but you likely mean **Indian Poker** (the card-on-forehead game) or a typo for **Face Poker** (live video poker). For Android, here are the best real poker apps:

- **Face Poker: Live Video Poker** – A free live video poker game where you play face-to-face with friends online and meet players worldwide [1].  
- **Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker** – The #1 free Texas Hold’em game with 3,000,000 free chips, live chat, and global play [2][3].  
- **EasyPoker** – A face-to-face poker app designed for playing IRL with friends using your phone as a digital poker set (no chips needed) [5].  
- **Poker Heat™ Texas Holdem Poker** – A Vegas-style live Texas Hold’em app with epic leagues and VIP competition [6].

If you specifically want the **Indian Poker** (card-on-forehead) game, it’s usually played physically with real cards; no dedicated Android app exists for it yet, though rules are available online [9]. For live video poker with friends, **Face Poker** or **Poker Face** are your best Android choices.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The card game you’re describing is **Cabo** (also known as **Cabo Card Game**), a memory-based game where players start with four face-down cards and **cannot look at any of them** except their bottom two cards once at the beginning [3].

For Android, the closest available app that matches this “can’t see your own cards” mechanic is **Card Thief** [1]. While not identical to Cabo, it’s a solitaire-style stealth card game where you transform your deck into a shadowy world of heists, and you often play without seeing all your cards clearly, relying on strategy and memory [1].

However, if you’re looking for a direct mobile version of **Cabo**, there is **no official Cabo app on Google Play Store** as of now. You may need to play it physically with friends or check for unofficial fan-made versions.

**Recommended Android card game apps** with similar memory/stealth elements:
- **Card Thief** – Stealth solitaire card game with hidden information and no internet required [1]
- **Marvel Snap** – Fast-paced card battler where some cards are hidden or revealed strategically [7]
- **Star Realms / Hero Realms** – Deck-building games with hidden card mechanics [6]

If you want to play **Cabo** digitally, consider searching for “Cabo card game” on Google Play, but be cautious of unofficial or low-quality clones.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The Android card game you’re describing is **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros.), where one player holds the phone to their forehead like a headband while friends give clues to guess the word shown on the screen [2][10].

A very similar and also popular Android app is **Charades & Headbands Game** (by Art Game Studio), which uses the same “forehead” mechanic: you place the device on your forehead, and friends act, sing, or mime to help you guess the word [1][5].

Both games are free on Google Play and fit your description of holding a “card” (displayed on the phone) to your forehead while guessing from friends’ clues.

- **Heads Up!** – Official app by Warner Bros., with many themed decks (celebrities, animals, accents, etc.) [2][10]  
- **Charades & Headbands Game** – Alternative app with 60+ charades decks and custom deck creation [1][5]

If you want the most widely known version, download **Heads Up!**; if you want more variety and custom decks, try **Charades & Headbands Game**.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
No single-player offline Android app exists for the traditional card game **Bluff** (also known as *Cockroach Poker*), because it is inherently a **multiplayer** game requiring opponents to call your bluff.

However, if you want a **single-player, offline, Android card game with bluffing/deception mechanics**, here are the best real apps available on Google Play Store:

| App Name | Why It Fits Your Request |
|----------|--------------------------|
| **Night of the Full Moon** | Vertical, offline, single-player card game with strategy and deception elements. Free to try one character; full game unlocks with a one-time payment. No ads or forced microtransactions [2]. |
| **Void Tyrant** | Offline single-player deck-builder with strategic bluffing-like decisions. Highly recommended for solo card gamers [2]. |
| **Card Quest** | Single-player, offline card RPG with puzzle-like strategy. Great for solo play without needing opponents [2]. |
| **Pirate Outlaws** | Vertical, offline, single-player card game with resource management and bluffing tactics. Free to try, cheap to unlock [2]. |
| **Underhand** | Completely free, offline, single-player resource management game where you lead a cult to summon a god. Beautifully drawn and short [2]. |

**Note:** The app **"Bluff - Cards Game"** by Oneiric Gaming [1] and **"Bluff"** by Floating Ninja Games [3] are **multiplayer-only** and require online opponents to play, so they do **not** meet your "single-player offline" requirement.

For a true solo bluffing experience, **Night of the Full Moon** is the top recommendation.
